proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1 – Precondition Propagation (prove guard correctness)
(declare-const args_len Int)        ; length of the raw argv array (≥0)
(declare-const has_reason_flag Int) ; 1 if "--reason" present, 0 otherwise
; -------------------------------------------------
; Precondition: args is a well‑formed array (non‑negative length)
(assert (>= args_len 0))
; Transition: getFlag returns 0 when the flag is missing, 1 otherwise
(assert (= has_reason_flag (ite (>= args_len 0) 0 1))) ; simplify: we model the *missing* case
; The function stores the result in `reason`
(declare-const reason Int)           ; 0 = falsy (undefined/empty), 1 = truthy
(assert (= reason has_reason_flag))
; -------------------------------------------------
; Property to prove: if reason is falsy (0) then the guard `!reason` is true.
; Negate the property → seek a model where reason = 0 **and** (!reason) = false
(assert (= reason 0))               ; reason is falsy
(assert (not (= reason 0)))         ; negation of `!reason` (i.e. reason ≠ 0)
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat → the guard always holds whenever reason is falsy
